stem: The perimeter of an equilateral triangle is 561 centimeters. The three vertices of the triangle lie on a circle. The radius of the circle is k√3 centimeters. What is the value of k?
rationale: Each side of the equilateral triangle has length 561/3 = 187 cm. Drop a perpendicular from the center O to a side, meeting at midpoint M of side AB. Then AM = 187/2. In right triangle AMO, angle OAM = 30°, so AO is the hypotenuse and AM = AO · cos 30° = AO · (√3/2). With AO = k√3, AM = k√3 · √3/2 = 3k/2. Setting 3k/2 = 187/2 gives k = 187/3 ≈ 62.33.
